passages.passage_text:
Directions (Q.101-110): Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below it.
Given here are the conditions to become the members of a Residents' Welfare Club. A person must:
1) be in the age group of 18-50 years as on August 3, 1993.
2) be drawing a minimum salary of Rs. 1 lakh per annum.
3) own a house.
4) not have a family of more than four members including himself.
5) have exhibited excellence in any field of arts and sports.
However, if a person:
6) satisfies all the conditions except (3) above, the case may be referred to the Club Secretary; and
7) satisfies all the conditions except (4) above, but draws a salary of more than Rs. 2 lakh per annum, the case may be referred to the Club President.
In each of the questions given below, one case is given along with facts. You have to read the information provided for each person in the questions carefully and decide which of the following course of actions can be taken regarding each candidate. Mark answer
(1) if the person is eligible to be a member;
(2) if the person cannot be allowed to the membership;
(3) if the data provided is inadequate;
(4) if the case is to be referred to the Club Secretary; and
(5) if the case is to be referred to the Club President.

Directions (Q. 111-115): Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below it.
The following are the qualifications necessary for the recruitment of a Librarian in State University.
The candidate must:
1) have a Master's degree in Library Science with at least 55% marks or its equivalent grade and a consistently good academic record.
2) have a one-year specialisation or Master's Degree in an area of Information Technology / Archives and Manuscript-keeping.
3) have at least ten years' experience as a Deputy Librarian in a University.
4) bear an evidence of innovative library service and organisation of published work.
In the case of a candidate who
5) has 15 years' experience as a College Librarian, the case may be referred to the Vice-Chancellor.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: A 27-year-old Indian, Naresh Saini is an unemployed graduate of Delhi since 1984. He has a family income of more than Rs. 15,000 per month, is a bachelor and lives alone in a house owned by him with his parents and grandparents (all alive). He is a Senior Manager in a private company and has exhibited excellence in Lawn Tennis. He has won 'Senior Singles' Trophy in badminton. He is 25 years of age.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Pran Chaturvedi working in Haryana State Corporation, Delhi for 9 years and has passed Senior Secondary from Delhi University. He is 35 years old, works as an official in a company receiving Rs 10,000 per month. She completed 10 years of service in the company on 9th January, 1992.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Maninder Vohra, aged 33 years, is an unemployed graduate from Delhi and his income is Rs. 55,000 annually and is living in Delhi for 12 years now.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Inderjeet Kohli, an Indian citizen, has no dealership in any oil company nor has any close relatives doing the same job. His family income is Rs. 15,000 per month.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Kuldeep Sanjay Rana, an I.A.S. officer, draws a salary of Rs 6,500 per month. His date of birth is 15th June, 1964. He holds a certificate of being the best player in his college. He lives with his wife and a daughter. He stays in government accommodation.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Ms. Sungeeta Seini, living with her mother and father, is a college professor earning Rs. 8,500 monthly. The house she lives in is on her name. She has a good record in the field of fine arts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Mr. Ashok Garg is 30 years old. He lives in a rented house owned by him with his parents and grandparents (all alive). He is a Senior Manager in a private company and has exhibited excellence in Lawn Tennis. He has won 'Senior Singles' Trophy in badminton. He is 25 years of age.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Mr. Kannadath completed 52 years in April 1991. He stays in his own house along with his wife of three sons. He works as a supervisor in a company and draws Rs 15,000 per annum. He has exhibited excellence in golf.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Mr. Virendra is 35 years old and is a government superintendent in a tourism department. He lives as a tenant with his wife and two sons. He draws an annual salary of Rs 8,700. He holds a certificate of excellence in sports as a Tennis player. He won first prize in the state championship.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Mr. Rajan Khanua was born in October 1958. He is the Chairman of Company and draws Rs 1,05,900 per annum. He has been the captain of the Basketball team of his office and lives in his own house with his wife.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Mr. Virk Chandrawat is 43 years old. He lives alone in his own house. He works as a supervisor in a textile mill and earns Rs. 9,000 every month.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Mr. Ajay Dhanugiar is a Manager in a transport and draws a salary of Rs. 2,08,000 per annum. He lives in a flat owned by him, along with his parents, wife and a son. He holds a certificate of excellence in Tennis. His date of birth is 16th November, 1968.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Mr. Prabhat Singh lives in his own house with his wife and a daughter. He gets a monthly salary of Rs. 7,500. He is a good painter and owns a gallery. He is 37 years of age.
